facts.fact: Long-horizon tasks expose planning drift across many intermediate steps.
Failures can compound when tool or test feedback is missed.
Explicit state tracking helps reduce loss of task intent.
Frequent verification or tests are practical mitigations.
Claims about autonomy should be stated with uncertainty.
LLM agents are limited by finite and shifting context windows.
Summaries can help but may omit important details.
Explicit state tracking can preserve goals and decisions.
Retrieval must be disciplined to avoid stale or irrelevant context.
Context practices should be verified against task outcomes.
Tool schemas can be brittle when arguments are ambiguous or underspecified.
Agents should inspect execution feedback before continuing.
Retries should distinguish transient errors from invalid tool calls.
Tests and validation reduce tool-related failures.
Strong claims should cite tool logs, evaluations, or other evidence.
